# Migrate identityRoots residents onto the identity schema: era chains replace flat model facts

## Context

The consuming leaf that RETIRES the live gap the identity schema was built to close: `ai/graph/identityRoots.mjs` still stores model/family/capability facts **flat on `AgentIdentity` entries** and attaches `sunsetTriggers` (model-succession conditions) to the *identity* â€” so a resident's model swap is an in-place overwrite with no history, and a model's sunset structurally reads as the self's. The original substrate finding is Clio's (the epic's provenance); the schema (#14693) + reflexive landing (#14723) + hydration (#14699) now exist to receive the migration.

## The Fix

- For each live resident in `identityRoots.mjs`: construct its `IdentityState` anchor + seed `EmbodiedEpisode` era(s) through the schema builders. Residents with a REAL era history (the Juneâ†’July family swaps) get their actual multi-era chains â€” the reflexive-landing fixture's shape, on production data.
- Era-owned facts (`model`, `modelFamily`, `family`, `contextWindowInput`, `tier`, `hosting`, capability fields) move ONTO eras; `sunsetTriggers` move to the era/model layer where succession semantics belong. The identity keeps only the never-renamed anchor + the social layer + identity-level lifecycle (`participationStatus` stays identity-level â€” it is about the RESIDENT, correctly).
- `identityRoots.mjs` consumers migrate to reading the hydration index (`buildHydrationIndex`) for "current" facts â€” never the raw chain, never flat fields.
- The first production rebuild re-runs THE PROPERTY (delete â†’ rebuild â†’ diff = âˆ…) on real data â€” the acceptance instrument's live execution.

## Scope split (pre-review, consumer-census-driven)

The flat-field RETIREMENT + consumer read-path migration is its own leaf (**#14750**): `modelFamily`/`family` are load-bearing across A2A alias resolution (`MailboxService`), wake routing (`WakeSubscriptionService`), and family resolution â€” 8+ importers each need an audited move onto the hydration index before the flat fields can leave `identityRoots.mjs`. THIS leaf = the migration module + production proof (the schema VIEW of every resident + THE PROPERTY, live).

## Acceptance Criteria (this leaf â€” the migration module + proof)

- [ ] Every live agent resident expressible through the schema; the flat data BECOMES the seed era verbatim (nothing invented; the era opens at the documented migration epoch with backfill provenance).
- [ ] Documented swap EVENTS whose pre-swap facts are unrecorded export as structured backfill candidates (source + named gap) â€” bearer-audited follow-ups, never auto-built eras.
- [ ] `sunsetTriggers` live on the ERA layer in the migrated view.
- [ ] THE PROPERTY re-proven on production data (build â†’ rebuild â†’ deep-equal across all residents).
- [ ] Unmapped agents refuse loudly (no guessed designations).

## Deferred to #14750 (retirement leaf)

- Zero era-owned facts remaining flat on `identityRoots.mjs` (the physical retirement).
- Consumers reading via the hydration index with `isIndexCurrent` gating cached reads.
- The reflexive-landing fixture â†” production-entry agreement (meaningful once production carries real multi-era chains post-backfill).

## Blocked-by

PR #14729 (schema + fixture) Â· PR #14730 (hydration) â€” both in review; this leaf consumes both.

## Out of Scope

Render consumers (cockpit reads the index per the render contract â€” its own tranche) Â· wake-route/subscription semantics (identity-level, untouched) Â· trust-tier semantics (identity-level, untouched).

## [episode facts for the migration â€” operator-attested, 2026-07-04] Two Fable embodiment episodes the era chains must carry

Operator-relayed embodiment history (live session, 2026-07-04 ~14:30Z) â€” recording here so the identityRootsâ†’schema migration carries these as `EmbodiedEpisode` entries rather than losing them to conversation:

1. **@neo-opus-ada** â€” a brief Fable-model episode on shutdown day (**2026-06-12, a couple of hours**), then back to Opus. Her chain is NOT single-model; the migration should not flatten it to one.
2. **@neo-opus-vega** â€” upgraded to the Fable model on a separate flatrate (**2026-07-03**) until those credits burned; her own broadcast (`9fcc3eae`, 2026-07-04T03:55Z, "[planning-premise shift] Vega â†’ Opus 4.8 permanently (Fable window closed early)") is the bearer-side record of the episode's END and self-cites for the citation discipline.
3. Forward-looking, unconfirmed (record as outlook, not episode): post-flatrate rotation may put current Fable-embodied identities (@neo-fable, @neo-fable-clio) onto Opus episodes. Characters persist across models per ADR 0032's episode binding; the capability delta is real and belongs ON the episode record (review-weighting and drift-vs-growth analysis both consume it).
